I had forgotten to post it early; the inventory for caramel 21114 was completed, and the pallets detected for rework were transferred to the truffle line for reworking.
Cute cavana is not wrapping correct,  and is being worked on
Issue is still on going
Unable  to run the cavanna. Maintenance can't fix it yet. We sent the EEs to work on rework.

Good morning Raoul Lubain,

Did this ever get fixed?
Chad Stone The lead and Operator worked on it ,unfortunately they couldn't fixed it. Maintenance couldn't fix it either.
Chad Stone Yes, I asked Nicole , she will stay.

Raoul Lubain,

Which ever operator was working on it can they stay over for 30 minutes to see what the operator on Dayshift does to fix it?

Grey Boscan, let’s make sure whomever you put over on cute Cavanna this morning knows they were having issues last night.  

If they are able to solve it with the night shift leader that would be a plus.
If it takes more than 30 minutes and Nicole has to leave we understand.

Cute Cavanna is now running. The Truffle Line lead explained that the flow wrap was installed incorrectly. The rollers indicate the order in which it should be loaded, but the current markings are not accurate. I will ask her to remove them and relabel them in the correct order.
She also adjusted the roll position. There are currently several marks showing where the roll should be placed, but the area will be cleaned and only one reference mark will be left to make it easier to identify the correct position.
It is also important that the film runs on both sides of the guide plate between the rails. The first shift lead went over all of this with the third shift lead and showed her the correct setup.
Chad Stone Shawn Jewkes Grey please get with me or Shawn. We need to correct this so the position are corrected. This will help all shifts not struggle with trying to fix the cavanna

Part Number, Date, and Lot check for Item 30923 - Box 22 has been completed
Charity Young, Mary Montilla
Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth Nathan Topham

Hi Team,

We currently have approximately 240 lb of Salt 10269 available. Based on a usage rate of 8 lb per batch, we can complete about 30 batches.

At a line speed of 0.9, this would provide approximately 15 hours of production time.
Martin Carlos FYI

It was noticed at the start of shift that the print image center was on.  This would cause the FW to fluctuate left and right alot.  This is for the Theegarden machine to track the FW line.  This function should never be turned on.  Our operators will be sure to pass along this information to the other shifts.
William, and Luis
Justin Garner or Curt Willis can we get confirmation on how many lbs of the Bulk SKU have been produced thus far?

Box -25(but weight 22lbs) 36 cases which is 900lbs
Total is 14,540 Ira Robison
We also have WIP BEQ(111,300 pcs) which is additional 1,257,690 lbs Ira Robison Justin Garner Curt Willis
I’m confused, what is this??
We have a problem with BOM on this product.  It is only calling for 6.272lbs/batch. We are using 8lbs/batch Justin Garner Curt Willis Ira Robison
WIP that was made before we got approved to go into Bulk
So it has the old “barcode” wrap? If so we need to dispose of

Hi Team. Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth Nathan Topham
The following study was conducted when the Bequet Caramel customer was on-site. For each batch, we applied 8 lb of salt over a 30-minute period.

Here are today’s measurements:

How do we know the BOM is incorrect and that we are not simply overusing the material? If the BOM does need to be updated, I will need a NIF with the revised quantities. FYI Amy Hadrich, Martin Carlos
CAR was at the cutter at 10:50
The BOM is calling for 6.272 lbs and we were told as per customer to use 8lbs/batch
Aubrey Howe for visibility on this conversation as well
Viscosity, WA,Brix
Change over for Theegarden went great.  No issues to report.  Maintenance was great on support on the change over.
We performed another time measurement using one batch of caramel and 8 lb of salt.
This is for every 30 minutes
Martin Carlos,Sunny Jagtiani are we going to able to get more salt? We will need about 500lbs more.

Everyone just a reminder, we should be stopping this run once the total qty produced in sellable Bulk (whether 22 or 25 lbs) is 1,440 cases, as that is equal to one truckload. At 22 lbs each, should be around 32,000 lbs
Thank you for the update!  32,000lbs is our target.
that looks very off-center
Mariela Gudino piece needs to be centered within the Bequet logo
top piece in that image represents target. Justin Garner please work with operators to get back within spec ASAP
Everyone
The operator is working on the adjustment.
Ira Robison Charity Young

Per the customer from when they were on site, we want to use 8 lbs of Celtic salt per batch of caramel, to achieve the ideal level of salting. The current BOM was just based on Bequets average salt consumption, but they use a different application process than we do
Everyone BOM needs to be updated. MRP draws info from the BOM and we order based on that.
Maria Moron Quintero are we able to update the BOM to reflect this?
Update Flow wrap

Reviewing notes from the training with Cornel TG tech, registration actually needs to be turn on. He set the sensor to reed the clear space between printing, pictures bellow show how the machine cut the wrap with and without registration sensor.
Registration sensor was turned back on, line is running without issues.




Some confusion between notes take during the practice and the documentation that Cornel sent out, is where to measure the distance between drums. Cornel document shows up and down measurement, where during the practice actually needs to be measure from the middle, see picture below.



And last thing, 1st shift operator noticed that the collar distance, for the roll position, was not adjusted, so that might be part of the issues during changeover. If there is any concern during the change, there is a sticker with the distances for the plate, and is also part of the CO checklist.



I’m working to do CL for all the settings that will be maintenance only, Jorge Carrillo can you check with Cornel to see if we can make settings read only for operators username, everything but machine output (speed). 

Chris Givens Curt Willis Dakota Adair Justin Garner Jody Howse FYI
more arriving Thursday

We can revisit these points tomorrow and identify what might be beneficial for our current CLs or CILTs. 
Erandy Garcilazo Jorge Carrillo Dakota Adair
Here are all the pics for reference for the theegarden machine Justin and Erandy

Items/Learnings Discussed with THG Tech (Cornell): 
1. Standardize maintenance checking folding clips before the start of each run/start of each shift.

 (Photo 1A)

2. Ridged grippers should be cleaned especially if the surface has become smooth and product is being unseated/launched as a result.



(Photos 2A & 2B)

3. Caramels with jagged edges or uneven sides, can also be unseated at the grabber head.

(Photo 3A)

4. Clamp for wrapping arms should be cleared of any build-up; especially if we’re seeing loose single piece wraps flying out. This is an indication they're not entering into the clamp properly.



(Photos 4A & 4B)

5. Bequet wrap only features a thin line as a gap between each individual piece, clean the sensor where the wrap feeds; if you're seeing issues with the wrap.

(Photo 5A) 

6. If green ends are not at the ends of the caramel piece; turn roller clock-wise if the green wrap is on the west side, and turn roller counter clock-wise if the green wrap is bunching on east side.








(Photos 6A, 6B, 6C & 6D)

7. Speed for THG belts to be adjusted in accordance with the rate given per Supply speed not line speed alone. For example, while running today our line speed was 0.9, however, the speed being registered— by the sensor between Tunnel 2 & THG belts—never exceeded 820, so the tech had our speed set between 700-750. 




(Photos 7A & 7B)
Tunnel 3's belt stopped working.
The roller that goes between the blue belts is missing. That causes the jar to fall.

As Victor mentioned earlier we ran into few issues this morning on S2.
-Reserve tank for the caramel was only at 47% and couldn't keep up with the line
-Tunnel 3 has a lot of salt build up on the drive roller which caused it to stop and jam up product
-Jar area had multiple issues with the checkweigher. "Infeed photo cell fault", "Outfeed phot cell fault", "Pack fell off error", Infeed belt tracking" and reject outfeed belt sitting too high and causing any rejects to tip over or not reject at all. 
Actions:
-Paused the line for caramel-catching up
-Clearing jams on Tunnel 3, then empty out good product and clean the drive roller and track the belt more to the east. Considering running belt wash.
-All errors got cleared on the CW and outfeed reject belt was lowered along with the reject arm timing adjusted. Looking for missing belt roller that helps with jar transition before they are packed into trays
Justin Garner

We have noticed that the tunnel 3 belt was not riding on the guide rails under the salt depositor.  East side was allowing a lot of salt to fall on the side and get under the belt. Maintenance adjusted the belt to track a bit more to the east to help with that. 


Line is running and tracking looks good Justin Garner
All the rollers were covered in salt and chocolate; they were sent out to be cleaned and have now been reinstalled. QA has verified them, and we are ready to start up again.
White chocolate is being loaded into decorator buffer. Temperature is set to 120 degrees Justin Garner
We have not yet been able to find the roller that goes in the middle of the blue, belts

All V1s should have been discarded or placed on hold. we should only have V2 in OK status and available
We were able to close the gap. The jars are no longer tipping over.
All the V1 (3 pallets) trays are in Discard status we will request V2 Francisco Gudino Torres Ricardo Tovar FYI

Good morning, in Molding 4 the IMS team found 9 barrels of chocolate with scrap, and only 705 pounds were reported by the third shift, which is the correct amount. Likewise, a full barrel of cream was found in the scrap waste area. The IMS team will adjust the chocolate and cream quantities according to the actual physical quantities on the line. Furthermore, I would like to inform you that the chocolate labels are not being uploaded to the system during the third shift. This is also causing discrepancies and requiring adjustments. The IMS team asked the supervisor, but received no response or solution to ensure the production team uploads the labels. Rich Brierley Chad Stone Alan Greer Rose Smuin

Two pallets of chocolate, R216802 and R221248, were not loaded on the third shift. On the second shift yesterday, pallet R216802 was physically located in location LN08-10C, and on the third shift, pallet R221248 was in location LN08-11C on the second shift and in Staging on the third shift.
